classification_guidance.goal: Classify market structure and regime fit for this bot, not predict event truth.
recommended_focus: Does this look like a gradual repricing market versus a discrete jump/news market?
Is the market family a good fit for the current bot logic?
Are there structural/risk reasons to avoid or manually review it?

markets.question: Will the Tampa Bay Lightning win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Tampa Bay Lightning win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

liquidity.explanation: More liquidity usually means easier entry/exit and less slippage.

history.explanation: Longer history makes the strategy test less fragile.

recent_move.explanation: Some recent movement is useful; dead markets are hard to trade.

volatility.explanation: Intraday volatility can create signals, up to a capped score.

quote_quality.explanation: Tighter spread is better when quote data is available.

price_zone.explanation: Prices away from 0/1 are often more tradable than near-certain outcomes.

movement_consistency.explanation: A bit of back-and-forth suggests the market is actually moving, not frozen.

markets.current_classifier_reason: Heuristic family: sports outright / repricing-friendly market | No external classifier configured
